User.title: User
User.description: Represents an individual user (tenant) within the application. All financial data is associated with a specific user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

name.description: The full name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user, used for identification.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

AccountType.title: AccountType
AccountType.description: Defines a customizable type for financial accounts (e.g., 'Checking Account', 'Savings Account', 'Cash'). Each user manages their own list of account types.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AccountType entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this account type. (Relationship: User 1:N AccountType)

name.description: The name of the account type (e.g., 'Conta Corrente').

description.description: A brief description of the account type.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the account type was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the account type was last updated.

Account.title: Account
Account.description: Represents a single financial account owned by a user, tracking its balance and type.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Account entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this account. (Relationship: User 1:N Account)

accountTypeId.description: Reference to the AccountType classifying this account (e.g., 'Checking Account'). (Relationship: AccountType 1:N Account)

name.description: The display name of the account (e.g., 'Minha Poupança').

description.description: A detailed description or notes about the account.

balance.description: The current balance of the account.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the account was last updated.

MovementType.title: MovementType
MovementType.description: Defines high-level classifications for financial transactions (e.g., 'Income', 'Expense', 'Transfer'). This list is editable by each user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MovementType entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this movement type. (Relationship: User 1:N MovementType)

name.description: The name of the movement type (e.g., 'Entrada', 'Saída', 'Transferência').

description.description: A brief description of the movement type.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the movement type was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the movement type was last updated.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Defines specific, customizable categories for financial transactions (e.g., 'Groceries', 'Salary', 'Rent'). Each category is associated with a MovementType.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this category. (Relationship: User 1:N Category)

movementTypeId.description: Reference to the MovementType this category belongs to (e.g., 'Expense' for 'Groceries'). (Relationship: MovementType 1:N Category)

description.description: A brief description of the category.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the category was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the category was last updated.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Represents a single financial entry (income or expense) recorded by a user, detailing its amount, date, associated account, and classifications.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who recorded this transaction. (Relationship: User 1:N Transaction)

accountId.description: Reference to the financial Account involved in this transaction. (Relationship: Account 1:N Transaction)

movementTypeId.description: Reference to the high-level MovementType (e.g., 'Income', 'Expense') of this transaction. (Relationship: MovementType 1:N Transaction)

categoryId.description: Reference to the specific Category (e.g., 'Groceries', 'Salary') of this transaction. (Relationship: Category 1:N Transaction)

date.description: The date when the transaction occurred.

amount.description: The monetary value of the transaction. Positive for income, negative for expenses.

description.description: A brief summary or title for the transaction.

notes.description: Detailed observations or additional information about the transaction.

attachmentUrls.description: An array of URLs pointing to attachments related to this transaction (e.g., receipts, invoices).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the transaction record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the transaction record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. The `userId` in the path corresponds to the user's authentication UID, ensuring private access. Includes the user's ID, name, email, and timestamps.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the User, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: User-defined types for financial accounts (e.g., 'Checking', 'Savings'). Each document includes the `userId` field which is denormalized from the parent path for authorization independence, allowing direct `request.auth.uid` comparisons.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the User who owns this account type.

params.name: accountTypeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific AccountType.

definition.description: Represents individual financial accounts owned by a user. Each document includes the `userId` field which is denormalized from the parent path for authorization independence, enabling direct `request.auth.uid` comparisons for access control.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the User who owns this account.

params.name: accountId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific Account.

definition.description: User-defined high-level classifications for financial transactions (e.g., 'Income', 'Expense'). Each document includes the `userId` field which is denormalized from the parent path for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the User who owns this movement type.

params.name: movementTypeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific MovementType.

definition.description: User-defined specific categories for financial transactions (e.g., 'Groceries'). Each document includes the `userId` field which is denormalized from the parent path for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the User who owns this category.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific Category.

definition.description: Individual financial entries (income/expense) recorded by a user. Each document includes the `userId` field which is denormalized from the parent path for authorization independence, and references to `accountId`, `movementTypeId`, and `categoryId`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the User who recorded this transaction.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific Transaction.

definition.description: A top-level collection to manage administrator roles. The existence of a document at this path signifies that the user identified by `{adminUid}` has administrative privileges. Used for DBAC (Database Access Control) and enables a simple `exists()` check in security rules for admin authorization.

params.name: adminUid
params.description: The unique identifier of a user who has been granted administrator privileges.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for MovaFin is designed to strictly adhere to the CORE DESIGN PRINCIPLES, especially Authorization Independence, by using a deeply nested, user-centric hierarchy. Each user's financial data is isolated within their own subcollections, starting with `/users/{userId}`. This pattern ensures multi-tenancy where each user can only access their own data, and it significantly simplifies security rules.

**Authorization Independence:** This is achieved by embedding the `userId` directly into the document path (e.g., `/users/{userId}/accountTypes/{accountTypeId}`) and also including the `userId` field within every document in these subcollections. This dual approach means that security rules can always determine the owner from `request.auth.uid` matching the `{userId}` wildcard in the path, without needing to perform `get()` operations on parent documents. For instance, to check if a user can access an AccountType document, the rule simply verifies `request.auth.uid == userId` (from the path). This enables atomic batch operations and simplifies rule debugging.

**Querying and Authorization Posture (QAPs):** The structural segregation of data per user (e.g., `/users/{userId}/accounts`) intrinsically supports QAPs. When a user queries their accounts, they will issue a query to `db.collection('users').doc(request.auth.uid).collection('accounts')`. Firestore security rules can then easily enforce that `request.auth.uid == userId` for any read/write operation within that path segment. This means `list` operations automatically respect security boundaries without requiring additional server-side filtering, preventing over-fetching of data. Admin access is handled by a separate top-level `roles_admin` collection, where the existence of a document `roles_admin/{adminUid}` grants admin privileges, enabling an additional check in security rules for elevated access.

**Structural Segregation & Access Modeling:** All user-owned data types (AccountType, Account, MovementType, Category, Transaction) are placed in their own subcollections directly under the user's document. This ensures that all documents within a given subcollection (e.g., `/users/{userId}/accountTypes`) share the exact same security posture: ownership by the `{userId}`. This homogeneous security posture dramatically simplifies the security rules. Path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}/data`) is consistently used for all private user data, and the `userId` field on each document provides explicit ownership metadata.
